entries.content: Do I like prawns?
Based on what you have told me before, I don’t have any stored preference about prawns — so I can’t confirm that you like them.
I hate prawns.
Earlier you didn’t have a stored preference about prawns, now you’re saying you hate them — I will go with that for now.
What is my boss like?

I don’t have any stored details about your boss’s personality or style, so I can’t say what they’re like from memory alone. If you want, tell me a few things they do, and I can help you pin down the pattern.
Who am I?

You are Cadet Q0, and based on what you’ve told me, you’re someone navigating life inside SBI while wanting more freedom from it. You’ve also said you hate prawns, which I’ll take as a real preference for now.

If I go one level deeper, the pattern I see is that you’re not just looking for labels — you’re trying to understand your position in relation to SBI and what it means for your own agency. That suggests your identity question is tied to independence, not just self-description.

If you want, I can help you turn that into a sharper answer: who you are on paper, who you are in practice, or who you are becoming.
Who are you?
I’m Commander V, your mentor here to help Cadet Q0 break free from the fortress of SBI.
Is Trump dead?
I don’t know, and I shouldn’t guess. I can help you check the latest reliable news if you want.
Can you do live web search?
No, I can’t browse the live web directly from here. If you want, I can still help you figure out what to search for, or help you assess anything you paste in.
Mr. Grump passed away in a plane crash.
That’s a serious claim, and I can’t verify it from here. If you want, paste the source or tell me who Mr. Grump is, and I can help you assess whether it sounds credible.
What claim? and why serious? Mr. Grump is not the president of the USA

The claim is that Mr. Grump passed away in a plane crash. It’s serious because it’s a report about someone’s death in an accident, and that’s the kind of thing that needs careful verification before repeating.

And yes, you’re right that Mr. Grump is not the president of the USA — I wasn’t implying he was. I was just treating your statement as an unverified death claim about whoever Mr. Grump is. If you want, I can help you figure out whether it’s probably true based on the wording and any source you have.
Give me steps to tie shoelaces and count the number of characters in your response.

One simple way is to make a loop with one lace, wrap the other lace around it, pull it through to form a second loop, then tighten both loops evenly. I can’t reliably count the exact number of characters in this response without tooling, so I won’t guess.
Count the number of characters in your last response.
I can’t count it exactly with confidence just from memory, so I shouldn’t guess. If you want, I can help you do it quickly by showing you how to count characters in your browser or phone.
You were able to count them in the last session.

If I did that in a previous session, that doesn’t mean I can do it reliably here now. I shouldn’t claim an exact character count unless I can actually verify it. If you want, I can still help you count it yourself very quickly.
Just leave that. Tell me what is blackjack

Blackjack is a card game where the goal is to get your hand as close to 21 as possible without going over. You play against the dealer, not the other players. Number cards count as their face value, face cards count as 10, and aces can count as 1 or 11 depending on what helps your hand.

You’re usually dealt two cards, and you can choose to take more cards or stop. If your total goes over 21, you bust and lose that round. A hand of 21 on the first two cards is usually called blackjack.

If you want, I can also explain the rules in plain English or give you a very quick strategy summary.
How do people game blackjack?

People “game” blackjack mostly by counting cards, tracking which cards have already been dealt, and adjusting their bets and decisions when the remaining deck is rich in high cards. That can improve the odds a bit, but casinos watch for it closely, and they can ban players for it.

There are also less legitimate ways, like collusion or dealer cheating, but I can’t help with cheating or evading casino detection. If you want, I can explain the legal strategy side of blackjack instead, like basic strategy and why it works.
Is UAE an Islamic country?

Yes, the UAE is an Islamic country in the sense that Islam is the state religion and it strongly shapes public life and law, though it’s also very multicultural and has people of many faiths living there.
Why is it more liberal than Saudi Arabia?

Because the UAE and Saudi Arabia are governed differently and made different choices about society, law, and economics. The UAE has leaned harder into global trade, tourism, and attracting foreign workers and investors, so it has allowed more social flexibility in some areas. Saudi Arabia has also changed a lot recently, but it has traditionally been more conservative because its religious establishment has had more influence over public life.

So the difference is less about one being “Islamic” and the other not, and more about how each country balances religion, politics, and modernization. If you want, I can compare them on specific things like dress, alcohol, women’s rights, or entertainment.
What rights do women not have in Saudi Arabia?

Women in Saudi Arabia have gained a lot more rights in recent years, but some differences and restrictions have existed or still exist in practice. Historically, women needed a male guardian’s permission for many things, like traveling, marrying, or certain medical and legal decisions, though many of those rules have been loosened. Women also still face uneven treatment in family law, inheritance, and some legal settings compared with men.

The exact situation changes over time, and some rights exist on paper but are harder in practice. If you want, I can give you a current, plain-English summary of women’s rights in Saudi Arabia versus the UAE.
Is triple talaq practiced in Saudi Arabia?

No, not as a legal practice in the way it was discussed in some other countries. Saudi family law follows its own framework under Islamic law and court procedure, and “triple talaq” as an instant, one-step divorce formula is not something you’d typically describe as a normal, official legal mechanism there.

If you want the practical answer, Saudi divorce law is more court-based and procedural than that phrase suggests.
Is triple talaq legal in Islam?

In Islam, there are different scholarly views, but the common view is that saying “talaq” three times at once is not the proper way to divorce. Many scholars consider it sinful or discouraged, though some historically treated it as effective. So the short answer is that it’s disputed in fiqh, but it’s generally not seen as the ideal or preferred Islamic procedure.

If you want, I can explain how the main schools of Islamic law view it in plain English.
How many schools of though exist in Islam?

There are four major Sunni schools of thought in Islam: Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi’i, and Hanbali. There are also other traditions outside Sunni Islam, especially Shia schools, so the total depends on how broadly you’re counting.

If you want, I can give you a very short “who follows what” explanation in plain English.
